words.definitionEn: a figure of a person or an animal in stone, metal, etc., usually the same size as in real life or larger
words.exampleEn: a bronze/marble statue

words.definitionEn: a hollow metal object, often like a cup in shape, that makes a ringing sound when hit by a small piece of metal inside it; the sound that it makes
words.exampleEn: A peal of church bells rang out in the distance.

words.definitionEn: a thin piece of wood that has fallen or been broken from a tree
words.exampleEn: We collected dry sticks to start a fire.

words.definitionEn: to fix something to something else, usually with a sticky substance; to become fixed to something in this way
words.exampleEn: stick something + adv./prep., He stuck a stamp on the envelope.

words.definitionEn: not moving; calm and quiet
words.exampleEn: A fallen tree floated in the still water.

words.definitionEn: to put something somewhere and keep it there to use later
words.exampleEn: store something, We do not have adequate space to store these documents.

words.definitionEn: a person that you do not know
words.exampleEn: There was a complete stranger sitting at my desk.

words.definitionEn: the quality of being physically strong
words.exampleEn: He pushed against the rock with all his strength.

words.definitionEn: long, thin material used for tying things together, made of several threads that have been twisted together; a piece of string used to fasten or pull something or keep something in place
words.exampleEn: a piece/length/ball of string

words.definitionEn: in a way that shows definite and serious opinions or beliefs
words.exampleEn: a strongly worded protest

words.definitionEn: a room where radio or television programmes are recorded and broadcast from, or where music is recorded
words.exampleEn: a television/recording studio

words.definitionEn: used to refer to a substance, material, group of objects, etc. when you do not know the name, when the name is not important or when it is obvious what you are talking about
words.exampleEn: What's all that sticky stuff on the carpet?

words.definitionEn: a type of solid, liquid or gas that has particular qualities
words.exampleEn: a sticky substance

words.definitionEn: a curve or turn, especially in a road or river
words.exampleEn: a sharp bend in the road

words.definitionEn: in a way that achieves your aims or what was intended
words.exampleEn: She had already successfully completed these courses.

words.definitionEn: to lean, or make something lean, in a particular direction
words.exampleEn: He bent and kissed her.

words.definitionEn: happening or done quickly and unexpectedly
words.exampleEn: News of his sudden and unexpected death came as a great shock.

words.definitionEn: to be badly affected by a disease, pain, sad feelings, a lack of something, etc.
words.exampleEn: I hate to see animals suffering.

words.definitionEn: to be convenient or useful for somebody
words.exampleEn: suit somebody/something, If we met at two, would that suit you?

words.definitionEn: right or appropriate for a particular purpose or occasion
words.exampleEn: a suitable candidate

words.definitionEn: to give a summary of something
words.exampleEn: This essay briefly summarizes some of our approaches.

words.definitionEn: a short statement that gives only the main points of something, not the details
words.exampleEn: a news summary

words.definitionEn: an amount of something that is provided or available to be used
words.exampleEn: Advances in agriculture increased the food supply.

words.definitionEn: to provide somebody/something with something that they need or want, especially in large quantities
words.exampleEn: supply something to somebody/something, The company supplied sports equipment to schools.

words.definitionEn: a person who supports a political party, an idea, etc.
words.exampleEn: a strong/loyal/staunch/ardent supporter

words.definitionEn: to be useful to somebody or improve their life in some way
words.exampleEn: We should spend the money on something that will benefit everyone.

words.definitionEn: used to show that you are almost certain of what you are saying and want other people to agree with you
words.exampleEn: Surely we should do something about it?

words.definitionEn: the outside or top layer of something
words.exampleEn: We need a flat, smooth surface to play the game on.

words.definitionEn: to continue to live or exist
words.exampleEn: She was the last surviving member of the family.

words.definitionEn: a period of time during which you swim
words.exampleEn: Let's go for a swim.

words.definitionEn: to change or make something change from one thing to another
words.exampleEn: switch (over) to something, We're in the process of switching over to a new system of invoicing.

words.definitionEn: a change in your body or mind that shows that you are not healthy
words.exampleEn: Symptoms include a headache and sore throat.

words.definitionEn: the part that sticks out at the back of the body of a bird, an animal or a fish, which the animal can move from side to side or up and down
words.exampleEn: The dog ran up, wagging its tail.

words.definitionEn: a natural ability to do something well
words.exampleEn: The festival showcases the talent of young musicians.

words.definitionEn: having a natural ability to do something well
words.exampleEn: a talented player/musician/artist

words.definitionEn: a long, narrow piece of material with a sticky substance on one side that is used for sticking things together
words.exampleEn: adhesive/sticky tape

words.definitionEn: money that you have to pay to the government so that it can pay for public services. People pay tax according to their income and businesses pay tax according to their profits. Tax is also often paid on goods and services.
words.exampleEn: to pay your taxes

words.definitionEn: to put a tax on somebody/something; to make somebody pay tax
words.exampleEn: tax somebody/something, His declared aim was to tax the rich.

words.definitionEn: connected with the practical use of machines, methods, etc. in science and industry
words.exampleEn: We offer free technical support for those buying our software.

words.definitionEn: a particular way of doing something, especially one in which you have to learn special skills
words.exampleEn: management techniques

words.definitionEn: to be likely to do something or to happen in a particular way because this is what often or usually happens
words.exampleEn: Women tend to live longer than men.

words.definitionEn: a shelter made of a large sheet of canvas, nylon, etc. that is supported by poles and ropes fixed to the ground, and is used especially for camping
words.exampleEn: to put up/take down a tent

words.definitionEn: something that is better
words.exampleEn: the better of the two books

words.definitionEn: to such a degree; so
words.exampleEn: I can't walk that far (= as far as that).

words.definitionEn: of or belonging to them
words.exampleEn: Theirs are the children with very fair hair.

words.definitionEn: the subject or main idea in a talk, piece of writing or work of art
words.exampleEn: the central/main/key/major theme

words.definitionEn: a formal set of ideas that is intended to explain why something happens or exists
words.exampleEn: theory of something, the theory of evolution/relativity

words.definitionEn: used to introduce the logical result of something that has just been mentioned
words.exampleEn: He's only 17 and therefore not eligible to vote.

words.definitionEn: to this degree; so
words.exampleEn: It's about this high (= as high as I am showing you with my hands).

words.definitionEn: used especially at the end of a sentence or clause to add a fact or an opinion that makes the previous statement less strong or less important
words.exampleEn: Our team lost. It was a good game though.

words.definitionEn: despite the fact that
words.exampleEn: Anne was fond of Tim, though he often annoyed her.

words.definitionEn: a passage in the neck through which food and air pass on their way into the body; the front part of the neck
words.exampleEn: a sore throat

words.definitionEn: in or into every part of something
words.exampleEn: They export their products to markets throughout the world.

words.definitionEn: in or into every part of something
words.exampleEn: They export their products to markets throughout the world.

words.definitionEn: held or fixed in position securely; difficult to move, open or separate
words.exampleEn: He kept a tight grip on her arm.

words.definitionEn: until
words.exampleEn: We're open till 6 o'clock.

words.definitionEn: until
words.exampleEn: We're open till 6 o'clock.

words.definitionEn: a metal container in which food is sold; the contents of one of these containers
words.exampleEn: Open the tin and drain the tuna.

words.definitionEn: very small in size or amount
words.exampleEn: a tiny baby

words.definitionEn: to give somebody an extra amount of money to thank them for something they have done for you as part of their job
words.exampleEn: Americans were always welcome because they tended to tip heavily.

words.definitionEn: one of the five small parts that stick out from the foot; any similar part on the foot of an animal or bird
words.exampleEn: the big/little toe (= the largest/smallest toe)

words.definitionEn: the soft part in the mouth that moves around, used for tasting, swallowing, speaking, etc.
words.exampleEn: He clicked his tongue to attract their attention.

words.definitionEn: being the amount or number after everyone or everything is counted or added together
words.exampleEn: Their total cost was $18 000.

words.definitionEn: the amount you get when you add several numbers or amounts together; the final number of people or things when they have all been counted
words.exampleEn: a total of something, You got 47 points on the written examination and 18 on the oral, making a total of 65.

words.definitionEn: completely
words.exampleEn: They come from totally different cultures.

words.definitionEn: the sense that enables you to be aware of things and what they are like when you put your hands and fingers on them
words.exampleEn: the sense of touch

words.definitionEn: to travel around a place, for example on holiday, or to perform, to advertise something, etc.
words.exampleEn: tour something, He toured America with his one-man show.

words.definitionEn: the activity of buying and selling or of exchanging goods or services between people or countries
words.exampleEn: international/foreign/global/world trade

words.definitionEn: to buy and sell things
words.exampleEn: trade in something, The firm openly traded in arms.

words.definitionEn: to express the meaning of speech or writing in a different language
words.exampleEn: translate something into something, He translated the letter into English.

words.definitionEn: the process of changing something that is written or spoken into another language
words.exampleEn: an error in translation

words.definitionEn: to take something/somebody from one place to another in a vehicle
words.exampleEn: to transport goods/passengers

words.definitionEn: to behave in a particular way towards somebody/something
words.exampleEn: treat somebody/something with something, to treat people with respect

words.definitionEn: something that is done to cure an illness or injury, or to make somebody look and feel good
words.exampleEn: The drug is used in the treatment of depression.

words.definitionEn: a general direction in which a situation is changing or developing
words.exampleEn: social/economic/political/demographic trends

words.definitionEn: something that you do to make somebody believe something that is not true, or to annoy somebody as a joke
words.exampleEn: They had to think of a trick to get past the guards.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody believe something which is not true, especially in order to cheat them
words.exampleEn: trick somebody, I'd been tricked and I felt stupid.

words.definitionEn: the true facts about something, rather than the things that have been invented or guessed
words.exampleEn: Do you think she's telling the truth?

words.definitionEn: a long, hollow pipe made of metal, plastic, rubber, etc., through which liquids or gases move from one place to another
words.exampleEn: He had to be fed through a feeding tube for several months.

words.definitionEn: an act of biting
words.exampleEn: The dog gave me a playful bite.

words.definitionEn: to write something using a computer keyboard or typewriter
words.exampleEn: How fast can you type?

words.definitionEn: used to say that something usually happens in the way that you are stating
words.exampleEn: The standard chips are typically used for databases and other business software.

words.definitionEn: a thick rubber ring that fits around the edge of a wheel of a car, bicycle, etc.
words.exampleEn: a front/rear tyre

words.definitionEn: unpleasant to look at
words.exampleEn: an ugly face

words.definitionEn: not having the skill, strength, time, knowledge, etc. to do something
words.exampleEn: a former soldier who has been unable to find work since the war ended

words.definitionEn: to use your teeth to cut into or through something
words.exampleEn: Does your dog bite?

words.definitionEn: not letting you feel physically comfortable; unpleasant to wear, sit on, etc.
words.exampleEn: uncomfortable shoes

words.definitionEn: clothes that you wear under other clothes and next to the skin
words.exampleEn: I never wear underwear.

words.definitionEn: without a job although able to work
words.exampleEn: How long have you been unemployed?

words.definitionEn: the fact of a number of people not having a job; the number of people without a job
words.exampleEn: an area of high/low unemployment

words.definitionEn: not right or fair according to a set of rules or principles; not treating people equally
words.exampleEn: They had been given an unfair advantage.

words.definitionEn: an organization of workers, usually in a particular industry, that exists to protect their interests, improve conditions of work, etc.
words.exampleEn: I've joined the union.

words.definitionEn: a thing that somebody has done successfully, especially using their own effort and skill
words.exampleEn: the greatest scientific achievement of the decade

words.definitionEn: used to say that something can only happen or be true in a particular situation
words.exampleEn: You won't get paid for time off unless you have a doctor's note.

words.definitionEn: different from a particular person or thing
words.exampleEn: Music is quite unlike any other art form.

words.definitionEn: not likely to happen; not probable
words.exampleEn: unlikely to do something, The project seemed unlikely to succeed.

words.definitionEn: not needed; more than is needed
words.exampleEn: They were found guilty of causing unnecessary suffering to animals.

words.definitionEn: not pleasant or comfortable
words.exampleEn: an unpleasant experience/surprise/task

words.definitionEn: a report or broadcast that gives the most recent information about something; a new version of something containing the most recent information
words.exampleEn: They will send you regular updates by email.

words.definitionEn: to make something more modern by adding new parts, etc.
words.exampleEn: an updated version of the app

words.definitionEn: unhappy or disappointed because of something unpleasant that has happened
words.exampleEn: I understand how upset you must be feeling.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody/yourself feel unhappy, anxious or annoyed
words.exampleEn: upset somebody/yourself, This decision is likely to upset a lot of people.

words.definitionEn: familiar with something because you do it or experience it often
words.exampleEn: used to doing something, I'm not used to eating so much at lunchtime.

words.definitionEn: worth a lot of money
words.exampleEn: My home is my most valuable asset.

words.definitionEn: how much something is worth in money or other goods for which it can be exchanged
words.exampleEn: to go up/rise/increase in value

words.definitionEn: several different
words.exampleEn: various types/forms/kinds of somebody/something

words.definitionEn: a form of something that is slightly different from an earlier form or from other forms of the same thing
words.exampleEn: version of something, the latest version of the software package

words.definitionEn: a person who has been attacked, injured or killed as the result of a crime, a disease, an accident, etc.
words.exampleEn: shooting/murder victims

words.definitionEn: to think about somebody/something in a particular way
words.exampleEn: view somebody/something + adv./prep., How do you view your position within the company?

words.definitionEn: a person watching television or a video on the internet
words.exampleEn: The programme attracted millions of viewers.

words.definitionEn: involving or caused by physical force that is intended to hurt or kill somebody
words.exampleEn: violent crime/criminals

words.definitionEn: a person who does a job without being paid for it
words.exampleEn: Schools need volunteers to help children to read.

words.definitionEn: to offer to do something without being forced to do it or without getting paid for it
words.exampleEn: He has been volunteering for 11 years now.

words.definitionEn: a formal choice that you make in an election or at a meeting in order to choose somebody or decide something
words.exampleEn: to win/lose votes

words.definitionEn: to show formally by marking a paper, raising your hand, using a voting machine, etc. which person you want to win an election, or which plan or idea you support
words.exampleEn: How did you vote at the last election?

words.definitionEn: to make something/somebody warm or warmer; to become warm or warmer
words.exampleEn: warm something/somebody/yourself, Come in and warm yourself by the fire.

words.definitionEn: to tell somebody about something, especially something dangerous or unpleasant that is likely to happen, so that they can avoid it
words.exampleEn: warn somebody, I tried to warn him, but he wouldn't listen.

words.definitionEn: a statement, an event, etc. telling somebody that something bad or unpleasant may happen in the future so that they can try to avoid it
words.exampleEn: I had absolutely no warning.

words.definitionEn: no longer needed for a particular process and therefore thrown away
words.exampleEn: the disposal of waste material

words.definitionEn: the act of using something in a careless or unnecessary way, causing it to be lost or destroyed
words.exampleEn: I hate unnecessary waste.

words.definitionEn: to use more of something than is necessary or useful
words.exampleEn: waste something, Stop wasting time and just get on with it!

words.definitionEn: to pour water on plants, etc.
words.exampleEn: to water the plants/garden

words.definitionEn: a large piece of a solid material that is square or rectangular in shape and usually has flat sides
words.exampleEn: The houses are made of concrete blocks with tin roofs.

words.definitionEn: to move your hand or arm from side to side in the air in order to attract attention, say hello, etc.
words.exampleEn: The people on the bus waved and we waved back.

words.definitionEn: an object such as a knife, gun, bomb, etc. that is used for fighting or attacking somebody
words.exampleEn: Modern nuclear weapons are much more destructive than either biological or chemical weapons.

words.definitionEn: to stop something from moving or flowing through a pipe, a passage, a road, etc. by putting something in it or across it
words.exampleEn: After today's heavy snow, many roads are still blocked.

words.definitionEn: to have a particular weight
words.exampleEn: How much do you weigh (= how heavy are you)?

words.definitionEn: located in the west or facing west
words.exampleEn: western Spain

words.definitionEn: any or every; anything or everything
words.exampleEn: Take whatever action is needed.

words.definitionEn: any or every; anything or everything
words.exampleEn: Take whatever action is needed.

words.definitionEn: at any time that; on any occasion that
words.exampleEn: You can ask for help whenever you need it.

words.definitionEn: used to express a doubt or choice between two possibilities
words.exampleEn: He seemed undecided whether to go or stay.

words.definitionEn: a period of time
words.exampleEn: for a while, I only stayed for a short while.

words.definitionEn: all that there is of something
words.exampleEn: The scheme would cover the whole of the UK.
